summary: Smoke-tested Pipa Jewellery backend (REST API endpoints) and frontend (homepage, login, admin dashboard, shop, add-to-cart). Most flows work, but signup endpoint returns 500 due to ObjectId serialization bug. Admin & customer logins, products, cart, wishlist, coupon validation, admin stats/users, RBAC all PASS.
passed: Frontend homepage renders hero, nav, banner
Frontend /login: admin login redirects to /admin (dashboard shows stats: Revenue/Orders/Customers/Products/Low Stock)
Frontend /shop loads product grid; add-to-cart button updates cart badge

failed.issue: POST /api/auth/signup returns 500 Internal Server Error for valid new users
failed.evidence: curl POST /api/auth/signup with unique email + 6+ char password -> HTTP 500. Backend log: 'ValueError: [TypeError("\'ObjectId\' object is not iterable")]' in fastapi.encoders.jsonable_encoder. Root cause: signup() returns the inserted `user` dict which still contains Mongo `_id` ObjectId. Login() correctly pops `_id` before returning; signup() does not.

action_items: Fix /api/auth/signup in /app/backend/server.py: pop '_id' from `user` (or build response dict without it) before returning. Mirror the pattern used in login() which does `user.pop('_id', None)`. This blocks the entire customer signup flow.
